zh.Egmond aan den Hoef, Bergen, North Holla: 荷蘭努-拉藍德伯根艾姆德 阿 丹 霍福
zh.lifeSummary: 當Mabilia I van IJsselmonde van Gelre於1160年出生在荷蘭努-布拉班特時，她的父親Hugo van IJsselmonde van Gelre30歲，母親Stephanie van Milly21歲。她和Wouter I Allardszn van Egmond至少有2個兒子和2個女兒。她去世於1187年7月12日在荷蘭努-拉藍德享年27歲。

zh-hans.Egmond aan den Hoef, Bergen, North Holla: 荷兰努-拉藍德伯根艾姆德 阿 丹 霍福
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Mabilia I van IJsselmonde van Gelre于1160年出生在荷兰努-布拉班特时，她的父亲Hugo van IJsselmonde van Gelre30岁，母亲Stephanie van Milly21岁。她和Wouter I Allardszn van Egmond至少有2个儿子和2个女儿。她去世于1187年7月12日在荷兰努-拉藍德享年27岁。

nameMeanings.name: van
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Anh, Thanh, Hung, Minh, Vinh, Dung, Hue, Huong, Lan, Quang, Tan, Thuy, Hong, Chi, Hang, Ho, Pok, Sang, Bai, Chin, Ching, Chu, Chuan, Dong.
Vietnamese (Vạn): from the Chinese surname 萬, see Wan 1.
Vietnamese (Văn): from the Chinese surnames 文 or 聞, see Wen 2 and 3, and also 5 below.

variants.name: Wan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Hong, Yi, Qi, Qiang, Wai, Yiu, Yuk, Chien-Min, Fang, Hin, Jichun, Li, Man, Chung, Min, Yiping, Pak, Sik, Sung Man.  Vietnamese Tin, Bang, Da, Hien, Hoi, Lan, Minh Quang, Sau, Tich, Tung, Yaping, Yuet.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 萬, which means ‘ten thousand’ literally or ‘very great number’ figuratively: (i) from the personal name of Bi Wan (畢萬), an official in the state of Jin (located in present-day Shanxi province). He was said to be a descendant of Bi Gong Gao (known as Duke of Bi), the 15th son of the virtuous King Wen of Zhou (reined 1152–1056  BC ). Bi Wan was later granted the fiefdom of Wei (魏), which in 403  BC  became the state of Wei (located in present-day Shanxi, Shaanxi, Henan and Hebei provinces). Some of his descendants adopted Wei (魏) as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his personal name, as their surname. (ii) from the personal name Wan (萬), style name of Rui Bo (芮伯, the Earl of Rui), an official of the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ), who was granted the fief Rui (located in Ruicheng in Shaanxi province). Some of his descendants inherited Rui (芮), the name of the fief, as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his style name, as their surname. (iii) from the placename Wan (萬), the name of a fief (located in Juxian in Shandong province) granted during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ) to an official in the ancient state of Chu. (iv) from the first element of the name of the Wan Si (萬俟) family, who lived in the downstream areas of the Yellow River during the Northern Qi dynasty (550–577  AD ). (v) from the second element of the name of the Tu Wan (吐萬) family, who are members of the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 完, meaning ‘complete’ in Chinese: (i) said to be borne by the descendants of Huan Zhui, a grandson of the Duke Huan of Song (ruler of the state of Song, died 651  BC ). (ii) from Wan (完), the name of Qu Wan (屈完), an official in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn Period (770–476  BC ). (iii) shortened form of the compound surname Wan-Yan (完顔), a surname from minority ethnic groups such as Juchen ethnic group in ancient northern China.

nameMeanings.name: Gelre
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German: possibly a variant of   Gelles  . This surname is rare in Germany.
Hungarian: from a pet form of the personal name Gellért (see Gellert ).
French (Gellé): variant of Gelé, a nickname from gelé ‘frozen’.

variants.name: Gelles
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Heinz, Erna, Kurt.
German: metronymic from the German female personal name Gele, a short form of either Angela or Gertrude (see Trude ). This surname is very rare in Germany.
Jewish (eastern Ashkenazic): metronymic from the Yiddish female personal name Gele, borrowed from German Christians (see 1 above), + Yiddish possessive -s.

nameMeanings.name: Mabilia
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Angelo, Carmine, Carmino, Domenico, Luciano, Nicola.
Italian: altered form of Mabilia, from the old female personal name Mabile, from Latin Amabilis.

en.lifeSummary: When Mabilia I van IJsselmonde van Gelre was born in 1160, in North Brabant, Netherlands, her father, Hugo van IJsselmonde van Gelre, was 30 and her mother, Stephanie van Milly, was 21. She had at least 2 sons and 2 daughters with Wouter I Allardszn van Egmond. She died on 12 July 1187, in North Holland, Netherlands, at the age of 27.

Elle a eu au moins 2 fils et 2 filles avec Wouter I Allardszn van Egmond. Elle est décédée le 12 juillet 1187, à Hollande-septentrionale, Pays-Bas, à l’âge de 27 ans.

Sie hatte mit Wouter I Allardszn van Egmond mindestens 2 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Sie starb am 12. Juli 1187 im Alter von 27 Jahren in Nord-Holland, Niederlande.

Lei avuto almeno 2 figli e 2 figlie con Wouter I Allardszn van Egmond. Morì il 12 luglio 1187, nel Noord-Holland, Paesi Bassi, all'età di 27 anni.

ko.Egmond aan den Hoef, Bergen, North Holla: 네덜란드 노르드-홀랜드 버겐 그몽 아아 든 호프
ko.lifeSummary: Mabilia I van IJsselmonde van Gelre(이)가 1160년 네덜란드 노르드-브라반트에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Hugo van IJsselmonde van Gelre(은)는 30세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Stephanie van Milly(은)는 21세였다. 그녀는 Wouter I Allardszn van Egmond(와)과 적어도 두 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1187년 7월 12일 네덜란드 노르드-홀랜드에서 27세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Mabilia I van IJsselmonde van Gelre родилась в 1160 г. в местности нурд-брабант, Нидерланды, её отцу по имени Hugo van IJsselmonde van Gelre было 30 лет, а матери, которую звали Stephanie van Milly, 21. У неё было по крайней мере 2 сына и 2 дочери от Wouter I Allardszn van Egmond. Она умерла 12 июля 1187 г. в местности нурд-холланд, Нидерланды в возрасте 27 лет.
